name: Plantain Porridge
intro: Proving that plantain can be used at all stages of ripeness, this recipe features green unripe plantain. You can of course use just-ripe plantain for a sweeter tasting porridge, but avoid using overripe plantain. The process of making this dish is similar to Asaro (page 54) and makes for a great alternative if you ever get bored with yam.

ingredients.item: green or just-ripe plantains (peeled and cut into bite-size pieces)

method: Heat the oil in a pot over medium heat and add the diced plantain and onions. Fry for 4-5 minutes over high heat, stirring occasionally. Add the stew base, vegetable stock cube, curry powder, chilli powder and salt to taste. Mix well, bring to a boil, then cover the pot and simmer over medium-low heat for 12-15 minutes until the plantain is soft. Add the meat substitute, tomatoes and spinach and cook for a further 3 minutes on high heat. Serve hot.
